questions.body:
Choose the events that occur in non-cyclic photophosphorylation but not in cyclic photophosphorylation.
I. Synthesis of ATP
II. Water splitting
III. Involvement of PS I
IV. Formation of NADPH
V. Phosphorylation occurs due to cyclic flow of electrons
Choose the correct answer from the options given below option.

choices.c: II, III, IV and V only

questions.subject: biology

questions.body: A police jeep (speed 90 km/h) is trying to overtake a culprit going on a bike (speed 72 km/h). The bike crosses a turning and the police jeep crosses the same turning 10 s later. Assuming that they move at constant speed, find how far away from the turning the police jeep will catch up with the bike.
questions.subject: physics

questions.body:
P, Q and R are three metals that undergo chemical reactions as follows:
P2O3 + 2Q → Q2O3 + 2P
2P + 3RO → P2O3 + 3R
3RSO4 + 2Q → Q2(SO4)3 + 3R
Observe the reactions and arrange the metals in the increasing order of their reactivity.
questions.subject: chemistry

choices.a: [curved graph showing s vs v with decreasing slope]
choices.b: [curved graph showing s vs v with increasing slope]
choices.d: [linear graph showing s vs v with different slope]

questions.body:
Refer to the given graph and select the correct option for the question that follows:

How much oxygen will be released to the tissues by blood on passing from lungs to tissues?

choices.a: 15 mL of O2/100 mL of blood
choices.b: 10 mL of O2/100 mL of blood
choices.c: 5 mL of O2/100 mL of blood
choices.d: 20 mL of O2/100 mL of blood

questions.body: In the above pressure (P) versus temperature (T) graph, which line represents the increase in density of an ideal gas?
questions.subject: chemistry
questions.skip_reason: Question requires interpretation of the P-T graph showing various lines (A through H) to determine which represents increase in density of ideal gas

questions.body:
Observe the given curve carefully and mark the correct statement.
(A) T1 is the melting point and dry ice changes to CO2 gas from t1 and t2.
(B) T1 is the boiling point and water vapours change to water from t1 and t2.
(C) T1 is the freezing point and molten copper(II) sulphate changes to solid copper(II) sulphate from time t1 to t2.
(D) T1 is the melting point and solid copper(II) sulphate changes to molten copper(II) sulphate from t1 to t2.

choices.a: T1 is the melting point and dry ice changes to CO2 gas from t1 and t2.
choices.b: T1 is the boiling point and water vapours change to water from t1 and t2.
choices.c: T1 is the freezing point and molten copper(II) sulphate changes to solid copper(II) sulphate from time t1 to t2.
choices.d: T1 is the melting point and solid copper(II) sulphate changes to molten copper(II) sulphate from t1 to t2.

questions.subject: chemistry
questions.skip_reason: Cannot solve without the temperature-time curve diagram

questions.body: Priya and Kartik wanted to study about diffusion among liquids. They took identical beakers and poured 100 mL of H2O in both beakers. Priya heated the water to 50°C but Kartik maintained the water at room temperature. They both added 5 drops of ink into the beaker, what will they notice?

choices.a: Colour of ink spreads faster in Priya's beaker.
choices.b: Colour of ink spreads faster in Kartik's beaker.
choices.c: Colour of ink spreads at the same rate in both beakers.
choices.d: In both the beakers, ink drops settle down at the bottom without spreading.

questions.subject: chemistry

questions.body: In animals, heart is the main pumping station, supplying and collecting blood from various parts of the body. In mammals, which of the following structures regulates the unidirectional flow of blood and found between left auricle and ventricle?
questions.subject: biology

questions.body: The chlorophyll molecules make use of visible radiations by absorbing wavelengths of light which are effective in photosynthesis. When a photon of light collides with chlorophyll molecules, it gets excited and when it comes back to ground state, the released energy is utilized in photosynthetic phosphorylation. Different wavelengths of light give different amount of light reaction products. Following are the absorption spectra of various wavelengths of visible light. Which of the following lights will yield maximum photosynthesis?
questions.subject: biology
questions.skip_reason: Cannot determine the correct answer without the absorption spectra graph shown in the figure

questions.body: Two particles A and B of masses 5 kg and 10 Kg are kept at a separation of 1 m. Both the particles are free to move. If the two particles are released from rest, then mark out the correct statement(s)

choices.a: Both the particles experience an equal and opposite force.
choices.b: Magnitudes of acceleration of both the particles are the same.
choices.c: Magnitude of acceleration of both the particles are different.
choices.d: Magnitude of acceleration of A is double of that of B.
